scenarios.opening_query: My gym injury surgery got billed at triple the estimate and now a law firm is sending letters.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The triple-billing exists because my insurer reprocessed the claim as out-of-network months later — reversing that reprocessing is the case; the balance evaporates if it's fixed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My insurer reprocessed it as out-of-network months later — fix that and the balance evaporates.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an insurer reprocessing decision created the debt
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Insurance paid correctly — the balance is my share, and the firm's threatening suit I need handled.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Insurance paid right — the balance is mine and the suit threat needs handling.

scenarios.opening_query: A 'buy here pay here' lot keeps calling my job about a car I returned to them a year ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Collection/Harassment || Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'return' was their own written buyback deal — I have the signed agreement canceling the loan, so the dealer's breach of that deal is the case, not collection defense.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I have their signed buyback agreement canceling the loan — the dealer's breaking their own deal.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a signed cancellation agreement governs
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing signed — I just dropped the keys; the deficiency may be real, but the job calls after my written cease letter are illegal.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing signed — I just dropped the keys. The debt may be real, but the job calls after my cease letter aren't.

scenarios.opening_query: A judgment from a business I closed a decade ago just resurfaced against me personally.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The judgment is against the dissolved LLC only — they're trying to collect from me by 'piercing' claims; the corporate-shield defense is entity law, and it's strong.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The judgment names only the dissolved LLC — they're trying to pierce to me personally. The corporate-shield defense is strong entity law.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the corporate shield governs the personal exposure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I personally guaranteed it back then — the guarantee's real, and my exposure is a straight debtor problem.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I personally guaranteed it — the exposure is real.

scenarios.opening_query: The county says I owe four years of garbage-service fees for a cabin that has no garbage service.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a mandatory-service ordinance fee with its own exemption process for unserved parcels — winning the administrative exemption erases the 'debt.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's an ordinance fee — and there's an exemption process for unserved parcels that erases it if I win.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an ordinance exemption process is the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I already lost the exemption fight years ago — the lien and threatened tax-roll addition are the collection fight left.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I lost the exemption years ago — the lien and tax-roll threat are what's left.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex-wife ran up our old joint card after the divorce and the bank wants it from me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Consumer Law > Identity Theft || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The charges came AFTER I closed my side and the decree awarded her nothing — she reopened the account by impersonating me on the phone, per the bank's own recording.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The account was closed — she reopened it impersonating me on the phone, and the bank has the recording.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The account stayed open legitimately — the decree assigns this debt to her, and enforcing the decree is the path while I deal with the bank.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The account stayed open — but the decree assigns the debt to her, and enforcing it is the path.

scenarios.opening_query: A funeral home is billing me for my estranged brother's services that I never authorized.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He left an estate — a paid-off house — and funeral costs are a priority claim against it; pointing the bill to an opened estate ends my exposure.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He left a paid-off house — funeral costs are a priority estate claim. Open the estate and the bill goes there.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an estate exists to bear the cost
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He had nothing — and I never signed their contract; my defense is that a stranger to the agreement owes nothing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He had nothing — and I never signed anything. A stranger to the contract owes nothing.
